question: What is the deep strategic logic behind Xinpinmu -- why NOW, and what does it mean for PDD's business model transformation?

findings.claim: Chinese manufacturing is structurally trapped at the bottom of the 'smiling curve' — the overall operating margin for China's industrial enterprises above designated size was just 5.31% in 2025, while branded consumer products globally achieve 45-70% gross margins.
findings.data: China NBS data (2025): Above-designated-size industrial enterprises had operating revenue profit margin of 5.31%, down 0.03pp YoY. Total revenue: RMB 139.2 trillion, total profit: RMB 7,398.2 billion (+0.6% YoY). For OEM/contract manufacturing specifically, net margins are typically 3-5%. By contrast, branded consumer products globally achieve 45-70% gross margins — a 2-3x gap. Private label fashion brands achieve up to 65% gross margins vs 25-35% for third-party sellers.

findings.claim: Costco's Kirkland Signature demonstrates the private-label margin premium: Kirkland products carry ~15% margins (nearly double national brands' ~7-8%), generate 32-35% of total sales from just 12.5% of SKUs, while pricing 15-20% below national brands for consumers.
findings.data: Costco CFO Gary Millerchip (Q2 2026 earnings): 'Kirkland Signature items typically offering 15% to 20% value compared to the national brand alternative, with equal or better quality.' Kirkland generates 32-35% of Costco revenue from ~500 products (12.5% of assortment). Kirkland margins ~15% vs national brands ~7-8%. Costco's overall operating margin (14.2%) outpaces Walmart (5.4%) and Amazon (5.1%). Analysts project Kirkland reaching 35-40% of sales by 2030.

findings.claim: The 'smiling curve' concept — with manufacturing trapped at the value trough while R&D/design and branding/marketing capture disproportionate value — directly motivated Xinpinmu. Chinese media frames this as PDD 'taking back pricing power' (拿回定价权).
findings.data: The smiling curve (proposed by Acer founder Stan Shih in early 1990s) shows manufacturing/assembly at the profit trough. For Chinese industrial cluster merchants: 'they possess world-class manufacturing capabilities but often earn only meager processing fees.' Xinpinmu is PDD's solution to this structural problem — 'the profit margins of midstream assembly and manufacturing will no longer be compressed but rather elevated by economies of scale and brand premium.' Ministry of Commerce researcher Hong Yong: 'through building brand advantages, products can form differentiation and premium capacity, shifting from price competition to value competition.'

findings.claim: Chinese companies that successfully moved up the smiling curve — Anta, Huawei, Xiaomi — demonstrate the feasibility but also the long timeframe required. Only 2 Chinese brands (Huawei at $117.8B, Xiaomi) made the 2026 Interbrand Global Top 100.
findings.data: Anta: transformed from Jinjiang OEM factory to Official Olympic Committee sportswear supplier. Huawei: brand value $117.8B, top-10 globally in 2026 World Brand 500. Xiaomi: $131.5B market cap, pursuing 'high-end strategy' as core direction. However, 78 Chinese companies in World Brand 500 averaged only $29.2B per brand vs US levels — 'large but not strong' pattern persists. Only 2 Chinese brands in Interbrand Global 100.
